org.apache.kafka.common.utils.ByteUtils;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.CloseableIterator;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Crc32C;\n\nimport java.io.IOException;\nimport java.io.InputStream;\nimport java.nio.BufferUnderflowException;\nimport java.nio.ByteBuffer;\nimport java.util.ArrayList;\nimport java.util.Collections;\nimport java.util.Iterator;\nimport java.util.List;\nimport java.util.NoSuchElementException;\nimport java.util.Objects;\nimport java.util.OptionalLong;\n\nimport static org.apache.kafka.common.record.internal.Records.LOG_OVERHEAD;\n\n/**\n * Concrete implementation of {@link RecordBatch} for Kafka record format magic version 2 and above.\n *\n * <p><strong>Binary Layout:</strong>\n * This class handles the serialization/deserialization of the following binary schema:\n * <pre>\n * RecordBatch =>\n *  BaseOffset => Int64\n *  Length => Int32\n *  PartitionLeaderEpoch => Int32\n *  Magic => Int8\n *  CRC => Uint32\n *  Attributes => Int16\n *  LastOffsetDelta => Int32 // also serves as LastSequenceDelta\n *  BaseTimestamp => Int64\n *  MaxTimestamp => Int64\n *  ProducerId => Int64\n *  ProducerEpoch => Int16\n *  BaseSequence => Int32\n *  RecordsCount => Int32\n *  Records => [Record]\n * </pre>\n *\n * <p><strong>Compression:</strong>\n * When compression is enabled, the record data following the RecordsCount field is compressed as a single block.\n * Decompression is performed lazily when iterating over records via {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}\n * or eagerly in {@link #iterator()} to avoid resource leaks.\n *\n * <p><strong>CRC Computation:</strong>\n * The CRC-32C (Castagnoli) polynomial covers all batch data from the Attributes field onwards (excluding\n * PartitionLeaderEpoch which is not included in CRC computation to avoid recomputation on broker assignment).\n *\n * <p><strong>Compaction & Producer State Preservation:</strong>\n * During log compaction, this batch preserves base and last offset/sequence numbers from the original batch,\n * allowing brokers to rebuild accurate producer state. Empty batches may be retained to preserve producer\n * sequence information until a new sequence is written or the producer ID expires.\n *\n * <p><strong>Timestamp Semantics:</strong>\n * BaseTimestamp represents the timestamp of the first record (or delete horizon for tombstone batches).\n * MaxTimestamp reflects the maximum timestamp of retained records (for CREATE_TIME) or the broker-assigned\n * log append time (for LOG_APPEND_TIME).\n *\n * <p><strong>Batch Attributes:</strong>\n * <table border=\"1\">\n *   <tr><th>Bits</th><th>Field</th></tr>\n *   <tr><td>0-2</td><td>Compression Type (0=None, 1=GZIP, 2=Snappy, 3=LZ4, 4=Zstandard)</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>3</td><td>Timestamp Type (0=CreateTime, 1=LogAppendTime)</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>4</td><td>Transactional Flag</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>5</td><td>Control Flag (control batches for transaction markers)</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>6</td><td>Delete Horizon Flag</td></tr>\n *   <tr><td>7-15</td><td>Reserved</td></tr>\n * </table>\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong>\n * This class is NOT thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is accessed without synchronization.\n * Multiple threads must not read or write the same DefaultRecordBatch instance concurrently.\n * Thread-safe access requires external synchronization or per-thread instances.\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Lazy Decompression:</strong> Use {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)} to decompress\n *       records on-demand, reducing memory footprint for large compressed batches.</li>\n *   <li><strong>CRC Computation:</strong> {@link #isValid()} recomputes the CRC every time; cache results\n *       if validating the same batch multiple times.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Iterator Creation:</strong> {@link #iterator()} decompresses the entire batch immediately\n *       for regular iterator support, while {@link #streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)} defers decompression.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see RecordBatch for the interface contract\n * @see MemoryRecords for managing multiple batches in memory\n */\npublic class DefaultRecordBatch extends AbstractRecordBatch implements MutableRecordBatch {\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET = 0;\n    static final int BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int LENGTH_OFFSET = BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET + BASE_OFFSET_LENGTH;\n    static final int LENGTH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET = LENGTH_OFFSET + LENGTH_LENGTH;\n    static final int PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int MAGIC_OFFSET = PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAGIC_LENGTH = 1;\n    public static final int CRC_OFFSET = MAGIC_OFFSET + MAGIC_LENGTH;\n    static final int CRC_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET = CRC_OFFSET + CRC_LENGTH;\n    static final int ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH = 2;\n    public static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET = ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET + ATTRIBUTE_LENGTH;\n    static final int LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET = BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + BASE_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET = MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET + MAX_TIMESTAMP_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH = 8;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET = PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET + PRODUCER_ID_LENGTH;\n    static final int PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH = 2;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET = PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET + PRODUCER_EPOCH_LENGTH;\n    static final int BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH = 4;\n    public static final int RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET = BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET + BASE_SEQUENCE_LENGTH;\n    static final int RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH = 4;\n    static final int RECORDS_OFFSET = RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET + RECORDS_COUNT_LENGTH;\n    public static final int RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD = RECORDS_OFFSET;\n\n    private static final byte COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK = 0x07;\n    private static final byte TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK = 0x10;\n    private static final int CONTROL_FLAG_MASK = 0x20;\n    private static final byte DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK = 0x40;\n    private static final byte TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK = 0x08;\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    DefaultRecordBatch(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public byte magic() {\n        return buffer.get(MAGIC_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void ensureValid() {\n        if (sizeInBytes() < RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD)\n            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record batch is corrupt (the size \" + sizeInBytes() +\n                    \" is smaller than the minimum allowed overhead \" + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + \")\");\n\n        if (!isValid())\n            throw new CorruptRecordException(\"Record is corrupt (stored crc = \" + checksum()\n                    + \", computed crc = \" + computeChecksum() + \")\");\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the base timestamp of this batch, which serves as the reference point for record timestamp deltas.\n     *\n     * <p>All record timestamps are stored as deltas relative to this base timestamp.\n     * If this batch is empty or marked with the delete horizon flag, baseTimestamp may be set to\n     * -1 (NO_TIMESTAMP) or the delete horizon time respectively.\n     *\n     * @return The base timestamp value (may be NO_TIMESTAMP (-1) if the batch is empty or uses delete horizon)\n     * @see #maxTimestamp() for the maximum timestamp in the batch\n     */\n    public long baseTimestamp() {\n        return buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long maxTimestamp() {\n        return buffer.getLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public TimestampType timestampType() {\n        return (attributes() & TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK) == 0 ? TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long baseOffset() {\n        return buffer.getLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long lastOffset() {\n        return baseOffset() + lastOffsetDelta();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long producerId() {\n        return buffer.getLong(PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public short producerEpoch() {\n        return buffer.getShort(PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int baseSequence() {\n        return buffer.getInt(BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    private int lastOffsetDelta() {\n        return buffer.getInt(LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int lastSequence() {\n        int baseSequence = baseSequence();\n        if (baseSequence == RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE)\n            return RecordBatch.NO_SEQUENCE;\n        return incrementSequence(baseSequence, lastOffsetDelta());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public CompressionType compressionType() {\n        return CompressionType.forId(attributes() & COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return LOG_OVERHEAD + buffer.getInt(LENGTH_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    private int count() {\n        return buffer.getInt(RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Integer countOrNull() {\n        return count();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write the entire batch (header and records) to the provided ByteBuffer.\n     *\n     * <p>This method writes the batch starting at the buffer's current position.\n     * The position is advanced by the batch size. This is a shallow copy operation that\n     * does not decompress records.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The target ByteBuffer to write to\n     * @throws BufferOverflowException if the buffer has insufficient remaining capacity\n     * @see #sizeInBytes() to determine required capacity\n     */\n    @Override\n    public void writeTo(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        buffer.put(this.buffer.duplicate());\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write the entire batch (header and records) to the provided output stream.\n     *\n     * <p>This method writes the batch to the stream without decompression.\n     * The stream's position is advanced by the batch size.\n     *\n     * @param outputStream The target output stream to write to\n     */\n    @Override\n    public void writeTo(ByteBufferOutputStream outputStream) {\n        outputStream.write(this.buffer.duplicate());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isTransactional() {\n        return (attributes() & TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n    }\n\n    private boolean hasDeleteHorizonMs() {\n        return (attributes() & DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public OptionalLong deleteHorizonMs() {\n        if (hasDeleteHorizonMs())\n            return OptionalLong.of(buffer.getLong(BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET));\n        else\n            return OptionalLong.empty();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isControlBatch() {\n        return (attributes() & CONTROL_FLAG_MASK) > 0;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int partitionLeaderEpoch() {\n        return buffer.getInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    public InputStream recordInputStream(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n        return Compression.of(compressionType()).build().wrapForInput(buffer, magic(), bufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    private CloseableIterator<Record> compressedIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier, boolean skipKeyValue) {\n        final InputStream inputStream = recordInputStream(bufferSupplier);\n\n        if (skipKeyValue) {\n            return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n                @Override\n                protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n                    return DefaultRecord.readPartiallyFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n                }\n            };\n        } else {\n            return new StreamRecordIterator(inputStream) {\n                @Override\n                protected Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException {\n                    return DefaultRecord.readFrom(inputStream, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n                }\n            };\n        }\n    }\n\n    private CloseableIterator<Record> uncompressedIterator() {\n        final ByteBuffer buffer = this.buffer.duplicate();\n        buffer.position(RECORDS_OFFSET);\n        return new RecordIterator() {\n            @Override\n            protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n                try {\n                    return DefaultRecord.readFrom(buffer, baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n                } catch (BufferUnderflowException e) {\n                    throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\");\n                }\n            }\n            @Override\n            protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\n                return !buffer.hasRemaining();\n            }\n            @Override\n            public void close() {}\n        };\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Iterator<Record> iterator() {\n        if (count() == 0)\n            return Collections.emptyIterator();\n\n        if (!isCompressed())\n            return uncompressedIterator();\n\n        // for a normal iterator, we cannot ensure that the underlying compression stream is closed,\n        // so we decompress the full record set here. Use cases which call for a lower memory footprint\n        // can use `streamingIterator` at the cost of additional complexity\n        try (CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = compressedIterator(BufferSupplier.NO_CACHING, false)) {\n            List<Record> records = new ArrayList<>(count());\n            while (iterator.hasNext())\n                records.add(iterator.next());\n            return records.iterator();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Return a closeable iterator over records that skips the key/value/headers fields for efficiency.\n     *\n     * <p>This is useful for validation or operations that only need record metadata (offset, timestamp, headers)\n     * without accessing the full key/value content. For uncompressed batches, record data is still read but\n     * not exposed. For compressed batches, this defers decompression until iteration, saving memory.\n     *\n     * <p><strong>Caller Responsibility:</strong> The returned iterator must be closed to release decompression resources.\n     *\n     * @param bufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffers for decompression. For memory efficiency with large\n     *                       compressed batches, provide a buffer supplier that reuses buffers.\n     * @return A closeable iterator over records (with key/value/headers omitted)\n     */\n    @Override\n    public CloseableIterator<Record> skipKeyValueIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n        if (count() == 0) {\n            return CloseableIterator.wrap(Collections.emptyIterator());\n        }\n\n        /*\n         * For uncompressed iterator, it is actually not worth skipping key / value / headers at all since\n         * its ByteBufferInputStream's skip() function is less efficient compared with just reading it actually\n         * as it will allocate new byte array.\n         */\n        if (!isCompressed())\n            return uncompressedIterator();\n\n        // we define this to be a closable iterator so that caller (i.e. the log validator) needs to close it\n        // while we can save memory footprint of not decompressing the full record set ahead of time\n        return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, true);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public CloseableIterator<Record> streamingIterator(BufferSupplier bufferSupplier) {\n        if (isCompressed())\n            return compressedIterator(bufferSupplier, false);\n        else\n            return uncompressedIterator();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setLastOffset(long offset) {\n        buffer.putLong(BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, offset - lastOffsetDelta());\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setMaxTimestamp(TimestampType timestampType, long maxTimestamp) {\n        long currentMaxTimestamp = maxTimestamp();\n        // We don't need to recompute crc if the timestamp is not updated.\n        if (timestampType() == timestampType && currentMaxTimestamp == maxTimestamp)\n            return;\n\n        byte attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType(), timestampType, isTransactional(), isControlBatch(), hasDeleteHorizonMs());\n        buffer.putShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n        buffer.putLong(MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n        long crc = computeChecksum();\n        ByteUtils.writeUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET, crc);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void setPartitionLeaderEpoch(int epoch) {\n        buffer.putInt(PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public long checksum() {\n        return ByteUtils.readUnsignedInt(buffer, CRC_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    public boolean isValid() {\n        return sizeInBytes() >= RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD && checksum() == computeChecksum();\n    }\n\n    private long computeChecksum() {\n        return Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, buffer.limit() - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    private byte attributes() {\n        // note we're not using the second byte of attributes\n        return (byte) buffer.getShort(ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        DefaultRecordBatch that = (DefaultRecordBatch) o;\n        return Objects.equals(buffer, that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer != null ? buffer.hashCode() : 0;\n    }\n\n    private static byte computeAttributes(CompressionType type, TimestampType timestampType,\n                                          boolean isTransactional, boolean isControl, boolean isDeleteHorizonSet) {\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Timestamp type must be provided to compute attributes for message \" +\n                    \"format v2 and above\");\n\n        byte attributes = isTransactional ? TRANSACTIONAL_FLAG_MASK : 0;\n        if (isControl)\n            attributes |= CONTROL_FLAG_MASK;\n        if (type.id > 0)\n            attributes |= (byte) (COMPRESSION_CODEC_MASK & type.id);\n        if (timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n            attributes |= TIMESTAMP_TYPE_MASK;\n        if (isDeleteHorizonSet)\n            attributes |= DELETE_HORIZON_FLAG_MASK;\n        return attributes;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write an empty batch header (no records) to a ByteBuffer.\n     *\n     * <p>This is used to preserve producer state during log compaction when all records in a batch\n     * are deleted but the base/last sequence numbers must be retained for duplicate detection.\n     *\n     * @param buffer The target ByteBuffer positioned at the offset where the batch should start\n     * @param magic The record format magic version (must be 2 or higher)\n     * @param producerId The producer ID for idempotent/transactional batches\n     * @param producerEpoch The producer epoch corresponding to the producer ID\n     * @param baseSequence The base sequence number of the (now empty) batch\n     * @param baseOffset The base offset of the batch\n     * @param lastOffset The last offset of the batch (may equal baseOffset if only one record existed)\n     * @param partitionLeaderEpoch The partition leader epoch at the time of batch creation\n     * @param timestampType The timestamp type (CREATE_TIME or LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n     * @param timestamp The timestamp value (usually base timestamp or delete horizon)\n     * @param isTransactional Whether this batch is part of a transaction\n     * @param isControlRecord Whether this batch contains control records (transaction markers)\n     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if magic version is less than 2 or if timestamp is invalid\n     * @see #writeHeader(ByteBuffer, long, int, int, byte, CompressionType, TimestampType, long, long, long, short, int, boolean, boolean, boolean, int, int)\n     */\n    public static void writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                        byte magic,\n                                        long producerId,\n                                        short producerEpoch,\n                                        int baseSequence,\n                                        long baseOffset,\n                                        long lastOffset,\n                                        int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                        TimestampType timestampType,\n                                        long timestamp,\n                                        boolean isTransactional,\n                                        boolean isControlRecord) {\n        int offsetDelta = (int) (lastOffset - baseOffset);\n        writeHeader(buffer, baseOffset, offsetDelta, DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD, magic,\n                    CompressionType.NONE, timestampType, RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP, timestamp, producerId,\n                    producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, isControlRecord, false, partitionLeaderEpoch, 0);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write a complete batch header with metadata to a ByteBuffer.\n     *\n     * <p>This method writes the fixed-size batch header portion (typically 61 bytes for magic V2+)\n     * but does not write any record data. Record data should be written immediately after the header\n     * at the buffer's current position after this method returns.\n     *\n     * <p>The method computes and writes the CRC over the attributes field through the end of the\n     * record data (which may not yet be in the buffer when this method is called if records are\n     * written sequentially).\n     *\n     * @param buffer The target ByteBuffer positioned at the offset where the batch header should start\n     * @param baseOffset The offset of the first record in this batch\n     * @param lastOffsetDelta The delta from base offset to the last record's offset (used to compute last offset)\n     * @param sizeInBytes The total size of the batch including header and all records, with the {@link #RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD}\n     *                    constant subtracted (i.e., the size of the portion after the Length field)\n     * @param magic The record format magic version (must be 2 or higher)\n     * @param compressionType The compression type applied to records (NONE, GZIP, SNAPPY, LZ4, or ZSTD)\n     * @param timestampType The timestamp type for records (CREATE_TIME or LOG_APPEND_TIME)\n     * @param baseTimestamp The timestamp base value (or NO_TIMESTAMP if batch will be empty; or delete horizon if delete horizon flag is set)\n     * @param maxTimestamp The maximum timestamp of records (or for LOG_APPEND_TIME, the broker-assigned append time)\n     * @param producerId The producer ID for idempotent/transactional batches (NO_PRODUCER_ID if non-idempotent)\n     * @param epoch The producer epoch corresponding to the producer ID\n     * @param sequence The base sequence number (NO_SEQUENCE if non-idempotent)\n     * @param isTransactional True if this batch is part of a transaction\n     * @param isControlBatch True if this batch contains control records (transaction markers)\n     * @param isDeleteHorizonSet True if the delete horizon flag should be set (baseTimestamp interpreted as horizon time)\n     * @param partitionLeaderEpoch The partition leader epoch at the time of batch creation\n     * @param numRecords The count of records in this batch (0 for empty batches)\n     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if magic version is less than 2, if timestamp values are invalid, or if {@link TimestampType#NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE} is passed\n     * @see #RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD for the fixed header size\n     * @see #writeEmptyHeader(ByteBuffer, byte, long, short, int, long, long, int, TimestampType, long, boolean, boolean) for convenience method for empty batches\n     */\n    public static void writeHeader(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                   long baseOffset,\n                                   int lastOffsetDelta,\n                                   int sizeInBytes,\n                                   byte magic,\n                                   CompressionType compressionType,\n                                   TimestampType timestampType,\n                                   long baseTimestamp,\n                                   long maxTimestamp,\n                                   long producerId,\n                                   short epoch,\n                                   int sequence,\n                                   boolean isTransactional,\n                                   boolean isControlBatch,\n                                   boolean isDeleteHorizonSet,\n                                   int partitionLeaderEpoch,\n                                   int numRecords) {\n        if (magic < RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid magic value \" + magic);\n        if (baseTimestamp < 0 && baseTimestamp != NO_TIMESTAMP)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid message timestamp \" + baseTimestamp);\n\n        short attributes = computeAttributes(compressionType, timestampType, isTransactional, isControlBatch, isDeleteHorizonSet);\n\n        int position = buffer.position();\n        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_OFFSET_OFFSET, baseOffset);\n        buffer.putInt(position + LENGTH_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - LOG_OVERHEAD);\n        buffer.putInt(position + PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH_OFFSET, partitionLeaderEpoch);\n        buffer.put(position + MAGIC_OFFSET, magic);\n        buffer.putShort(position + ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, attributes);\n        buffer.putLong(position + BASE_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, baseTimestamp);\n        buffer.putLong(position + MAX_TIMESTAMP_OFFSET, maxTimestamp);\n        buffer.putInt(position + LAST_OFFSET_DELTA_OFFSET, lastOffsetDelta);\n        buffer.putLong(position + PRODUCER_ID_OFFSET, producerId);\n        buffer.putShort(position + PRODUCER_EPOCH_OFFSET, epoch);\n        buffer.putInt(position + BASE_SEQUENCE_OFFSET, sequence);\n        buffer.putInt(position + RECORDS_COUNT_OFFSET, numRecords);\n        long crc = Crc32C.compute(buffer, ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET, sizeInBytes - ATTRIBUTES_OFFSET);\n        buffer.putInt(position + CRC_OFFSET, (int) crc);\n        buffer.position(position + RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"RecordBatch(magic=\" + magic() + \", offsets=[\" + baseOffset() + \", \" + lastOffset() + \"], \" +\n                \"sequence=[\" + baseSequence() + \", \" + lastSequence() + \"], \" +\n                \"partitionLeaderEpoch=\" + partitionLeaderEpoch() + \", \" +\n                \"isTransactional=\" + isTransactional() + \", isControlBatch=\" + isControlBatch() + \", \" +\n                \"compression=\" + compressionType() + \", timestampType=\" + timestampType() + \", crc=\" + checksum() + \")\";\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Calculate the total serialized size of a batch with the given records.\n     *\n     * <p>This includes the batch header ({@link #RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD} bytes) plus the serialized\n     * size of each record with offset/timestamp deltas computed relative to the batch base offset\n     * and first record's timestamp.\n     *\n     * @param baseOffset The base offset for offset delta calculation\n     * @param records The records to include in the size calculation\n     * @return The total size in bytes including header and all records, or 0 if records is empty\n     */\n    public static int sizeInBytes(long baseOffset, Iterable<Record> records) {\n        Iterator<Record> iterator = records.iterator();\n        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n            return 0;\n\n        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n            Record record = iterator.next();\n            int offsetDelta = (int) (record.offset() - baseOffset);\n            if (baseTimestamp == null)\n                baseTimestamp = record.timestamp();\n            long timestampDelta = record.timestamp() - baseTimestamp;\n            size += DefaultRecord.sizeInBytes(offsetDelta, timestampDelta, record.key(), record.value(),\n                    record.headers());\n        }\n        return size;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Calculate the total serialized size of a batch with the given simple records.\n     *\n     * <p>Similar to {@link #sizeInBytes(long, Iterable)} but accepts SimpleRecord objects and\n     * assumes a base offset of 0 with offset deltas incrementing from 0.\n     *\n     * @param records The simple records to include in the size calculation\n     * @return The total size in bytes including header and all records, or 0 if records is empty\n     */\n    public static int sizeInBytes(Iterable<SimpleRecord> records) {\n        Iterator<SimpleRecord> iterator = records.iterator();\n        if (!iterator.hasNext())\n            return 0;\n\n        int size = RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD;\n        int offsetDelta = 0;\n        Long baseTimestamp = null;\n        while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n            SimpleRecord record = iterator.next();\n            if (baseTimestamp == null)\n                baseTimestamp = record.timestamp();\n            long timestampDelta = record.timestamp() - baseTimestamp;\n            size += DefaultRecord.sizeInBytes(offsetDelta++, timestampDelta, record.key(), record.value(),\n                    record.headers());\n        }\n        return size;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get an upper bound on the size of a batch with only a single record using a given key and value.\n     *\n     * <p>This is a conservative estimate for buffer pre-allocation purposes. The actual size may be\n     * smaller due to variable-length encoding of record fields. Compression overhead is not considered,\n     * so the actual compressed size may differ.\n     *\n     * @param key The record key (nullable)\n     * @param value The record value (nullable)\n     * @param headers The record headers array\n     * @return An upper bound on the serialized batch size in bytes\n     */\n    static int estimateBatchSizeUpperBound(ByteBuffer key, ByteBuffer value, Header[] headers) {\n        return RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD + DefaultRecord.recordSizeUpperBound(key, value, headers);\n    }\n\n    public static int incrementSequence(int sequence, int increment) {\n        if (sequence > Integer.MAX_VALUE - increment)\n            return increment - (Integer.MAX_VALUE - sequence) - 1;\n        return sequence + increment;\n    }\n\n    public static int decrementSequence(int sequence, int decrement) {\n        if (sequence < decrement)\n            return Integer.MAX_VALUE - (decrement - sequence) + 1;\n        return sequence - decrement;\n    }\n\n    // visible for testing\n    abstract class RecordIterator implements CloseableIterator<Record> {\n        private final Long logAppendTime;\n        private final long baseOffset;\n        private final long baseTimestamp;\n        private final int baseSequence;\n        private final int numRecords;\n        private int readRecords = 0;\n\n        RecordIterator() {\n            this.logAppendTime = timestampType() == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ? maxTimestamp() : null;\n            this.baseOffset = baseOffset();\n            this.baseTimestamp = baseTimestamp();\n            this.baseSequence = baseSequence();\n            int numRecords = count();\n            if (numRecords < 0)\n                throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Found invalid record count \" + numRecords + \" in magic v\" +\n                        magic() + \" batch\");\n            this.numRecords = numRecords;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public boolean hasNext() {\n            return readRecords < numRecords;\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public Record next() {\n            if (readRecords >= numRecords)\n                throw new NoSuchElementException();\n\n            readRecords++;\n            Record rec = readNext(baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n            if (readRecords == numRecords) {\n                // Validate that the actual size of the batch is equal to declared size\n                // by checking that after reading declared number of items, there no items left\n                // (overflow case, i.e. reading past buffer end is checked elsewhere).\n                if (!ensureNoneRemaining())\n                    throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, records still remaining in file\");\n            }\n            return rec;\n        }\n\n        protected abstract Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime);\n\n        protected abstract boolean ensureNoneRemaining();\n\n        @Override\n        public void remove() {\n            throw new UnsupportedOperationException();\n        }\n\n    }\n\n    // visible for testing\n    abstract class StreamRecordIterator extends RecordIterator {\n        private final InputStream inputStream;\n\n        StreamRecordIterator(InputStream inputStream) {\n            super();\n            this.inputStream = inputStream;\n        }\n\n        abstract Record doReadRecord(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) throws IOException;\n\n        @Override\n        protected Record readNext(long baseOffset, long baseTimestamp, int baseSequence, Long logAppendTime) {\n            try {\n                return doReadRecord(baseOffset, baseTimestamp, baseSequence, logAppendTime);\n            } catch (IllegalArgumentException e) {\n                throw new InvalidRecordException(\"Incorrect declared batch size, premature EOF reached\", e);\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to decompress record stream\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        protected boolean ensureNoneRemaining() {\n            try {\n                return inputStream.read() == -1;\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Error checking for remaining bytes after reading batch\", e);\n            }\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void close() {\n            try {\n                inputStream.close();\n            } catch (IOException e) {\n                throw new KafkaException(\"Failed to close record stream\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    static class This is used only for reading or\n * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},\n * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {","new_string":"/**\n * In-memory collection of Kafka record batches backed by a ByteBuffer.\n *\n * <p><strong>Purpose:</strong>\n * This class manages a contiguous buffer containing one or more {@link RecordBatch} objects,\n * providing iteration, filtering, slicing, and serialization capabilities. It is used throughout\n * Kafka for handling batches in producers, consumers, brokers, and administrative operations.\n *\n * <p><strong>Use Cases:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Reading Existing Batches:</strong> Use {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)} to wrap\n *       an existing buffer for reading.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Creating New Batches:</strong> Use {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)}\n *       or {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} for batch construction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Log Filtering & Compaction:</strong> Use {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}\n *       to filter records by retention policy during log compaction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Network I/O:</strong> Use {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel)} or\n *       {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.TransferableChannel, int, int)} for efficient data transmission.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Batch Structure:</strong>\n * The buffer contains a sequence of batches (one or more). Each batch encapsulates records with shared\n * metadata (compression type, timestamp type, producer ID, etc.). Batches are decoded lazily as they are\n * iterated; use {@link #batches()} to iterate over all batches or individual methods like\n * {@link #firstBatchSize()} to inspect specific batches.\n *\n * <p><strong>Decompression & Iterator Semantics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Streaming Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}\n *       defers decompression until records are consumed, reducing memory footprint.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Regular Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#iterator()} decompresses the full batch\n *       upfront, allowing iteration without resource management concerns.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Filtering:</strong> {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)} combines\n *       batch and record filtering, supporting log compaction and retention policies.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong>\n * This class is NOT thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is accessed without synchronization.\n * Multiple threads must not read or write the same MemoryRecords instance concurrently.\n * For concurrent access, wrap each thread's iteration with external synchronization or use\n * thread-local instances.\n *\n * <p><strong>Buffer Ownership & Lifecycle:</strong>\n * MemoryRecords does not own the backing ByteBuffer; the caller is responsible for:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Keeping the buffer valid during MemoryRecords' lifetime</li>\n *   <li>Releasing/deallocating the buffer when no longer needed</li>\n *   <li>Avoiding modifications to the buffer while iteration is in progress</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Lazy Batch Parsing:</strong> Batches are decoded on-demand during iteration.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Valid Bytes Caching:</strong> {@link #validBytes()} is computed once and cached;\n *       invalidate by creating a new MemoryRecords instance if the buffer changes.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Decompression Buffers:</strong> Provide reusable {@link BufferSupplier} instances\n *       to streaming iterators to reduce allocations for large compressed batches.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see RecordBatch for the batch interface\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for constructing new MemoryRecords\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch for the V2+ batch implementation\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"cache_creation_input_tokens":840,"cache_read_input_tokens":58973,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":840},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"c4612e00-4390-4b6c-8341-dfb9cd38c912","uuid":"1c724c83-8318-4a2c-be04-cb429d743200"}

It is used throughout\n * Kafka for handling batches in producers, consumers, brokers, and administrative operations.\n *\n * <p><strong>Use Cases:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Reading Existing Batches:</strong> Use {@link #readableRecords(ByteBuffer)} to wrap\n *       an existing buffer for reading.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Creating New Batches:</strong> Use {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, Compression, TimestampType, long)}\n *       or {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder} for batch construction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Log Filtering & Compaction:</strong> Use {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)}\n *       to filter records by retention policy during log compaction.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Network I/O:</strong> Use {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.GatheringByteChannel)} or\n *       {@link #writeTo(java.nio.channels.TransferableChannel, int, int)} for efficient data transmission.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Batch Structure:</strong>\n * The buffer contains a sequence of batches (one or more). Each batch encapsulates records with shared\n * metadata (compression type, timestamp type, producer ID, etc.). Batches are decoded lazily as they are\n * iterated; use {@link #batches()} to iterate over all batches or individual methods like\n * {@link #firstBatchSize()} to inspect specific batches.\n *\n * <p><strong>Decompression & Iterator Semantics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Streaming Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#streamingIterator(BufferSupplier)}\n *       defers decompression until records are consumed, reducing memory footprint.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Regular Iteration:</strong> {@link RecordBatch#iterator()} decompresses the full batch\n *       upfront, allowing iteration without resource management concerns.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Filtering:</strong> {@link #filterTo(RecordFilter, ByteBuffer, BufferSupplier)} combines\n *       batch and record filtering, supporting log compaction and retention policies.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Thread Safety:</strong>\n * This class is NOT thread-safe. The underlying ByteBuffer is accessed without synchronization.\n * Multiple threads must not read or write the same MemoryRecords instance concurrently.\n * For concurrent access, wrap each thread's iteration with external synchronization or use\n * thread-local instances.\n *\n * <p><strong>Buffer Ownership & Lifecycle:</strong>\n * MemoryRecords does not own the backing ByteBuffer; the caller is responsible for:\n * <ul>\n *   <li>Keeping the buffer valid during MemoryRecords' lifetime</li>\n *   <li>Releasing/deallocating the buffer when no longer needed</li>\n *   <li>Avoiding modifications to the buffer while iteration is in progress</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * <p><strong>Performance Characteristics:</strong>\n * <ul>\n *   <li><strong>Lazy Batch Parsing:</strong> Batches are decoded on-demand during iteration.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Valid Bytes Caching:</strong> {@link #validBytes()} is computed once and cached;\n *       invalidate by creating a new MemoryRecords instance if the buffer changes.</li>\n *   <li><strong>Decompression Buffers:</strong> Provide reusable {@link BufferSupplier} instances\n *       to streaming iterators to reduce allocations for large compressed batches.</li>\n * </ul>\n *\n * @see RecordBatch for the batch interface\n * @see MemoryRecordsBuilder for constructing new MemoryRecords\n * @see DefaultRecordBatch for the V2+ batch implementation\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {","originalFile":"/*\n * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. This is used only for reading or\n * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},\n * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.\n */\npublic class MemoryRecords extends AbstractRecords {\n    public static final MemoryRecords EMPTY = MemoryRecords.readableRecords(ByteBuffer.allocate(0));\n\n    private final ByteBuffer buffer;\n\n    private final Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches = this::batchIterator;\n\n    private int validBytes = -1;\n\n    // Construct a writable memory records\n    private MemoryRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(buffer, \"buffer should not be null\");\n        this.buffer = buffer;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int sizeInBytes() {\n        return buffer.limit();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int writeTo(TransferableChannel channel, int position, int length) throws IOException {\n        if (((long) position) + length > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"position+length should not be greater than buffer.limit(), position: \"\n                    + position + \", length: \" + length + \", buffer.limit(): \" + buffer.limit());\n\n        return Utils.tryWriteTo(channel, position, length, buffer);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Write all records to the given channel (including partial records).\n     * @param channel The channel to write to\n     * @return The number of bytes written\n     * @throws IOException For any IO errors writing to the channel\n     */\n    public int writeFullyTo(GatheringByteChannel channel) throws IOException {\n        buffer.mark();\n        int written = 0;\n        while (written < sizeInBytes())\n            written += channel.write(buffer);\n        buffer.reset();\n        return written;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * The total number of bytes in this message set not including any partial, trailing messages. This\n     * may be smaller than what is returned by {@link #sizeInBytes()}.\n     * @return The number of valid bytes\n     */\n    public int validBytes() {\n        if (validBytes >= 0)\n            return validBytes;\n\n        int bytes = 0;\n        for (RecordBatch batch : batches())\n            bytes += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n        this.validBytes = bytes;\n        return bytes;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public AbstractIterator<MutableRecordBatch> batchIterator() {\n        return new RecordBatchIterator<>(new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer.duplicate(), Integer.MAX_VALUE));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Validates the header of the first batch and returns batch size.\n     * @return first batch size including LOG_OVERHEAD if buffer contains header up to\n     *         magic byte, null otherwise\n     * @throws CorruptRecordException if record size or magic is invalid\n     */\n    public Integer firstBatchSize() {\n        if (buffer.remaining() < HEADER_SIZE_UP_TO_MAGIC)\n            return null;\n        return new ByteBufferLogInputStream(buffer, Integer.MAX_VALUE).nextBatchSize();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Filter the records into the provided ByteBuffer.\n     *\n     * @param filter                      The filter function\n     * @param destinationBuffer           The byte buffer to write the filtered records to\n     * @param decompressionBufferSupplier The supplier of ByteBuffer(s) used for decompression if supported. For small\n     *                                    record batches, allocating a potentially large buffer (64 KB for LZ4) will\n     *                                    dominate the cost of decompressing and iterating over the records in the\n     *                                    batch. As such, a supplier that reuses buffers will have a significant\n     *                                    performance impact.\n     * @return A FilterResult with a summary of the output (for metrics) and potentially an overflow buffer\n     */\n    public FilterResult filterTo(RecordFilter filter, ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        return filterTo(batches(), filter, destinationBuffer, decompressionBufferSupplier);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Note: This method is also used to convert the first timestamp of the batch (which is usually the timestamp of the first record)\n     * to the delete horizon of the tombstones or txn markers which are present in the batch.\n     */\n    private static FilterResult filterTo(Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches, RecordFilter filter,\n                                         ByteBuffer destinationBuffer, BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier) {\n        FilterResult filterResult = new FilterResult(destinationBuffer);\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(destinationBuffer);\n        for (MutableRecordBatch batch : batches) {\n            final BatchRetentionResult batchRetentionResult = filter.checkBatchRetention(batch);\n            final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = batchRetentionResult.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            final BatchRetention batchRetention = batchRetentionResult.batchRetention;\n\n            filterResult.bytesRead += batch.sizeInBytes();\n\n            if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.DELETE)\n                continue;\n\n            final BatchFilterResult iterationResult = filterBatch(batch, decompressionBufferSupplier, filterResult,\n                filter);\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = iterationResult.retainedRecords;\n            boolean containsTombstones = iterationResult.containsTombstones;\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = iterationResult.writeOriginalBatch;\n            long maxOffset = iterationResult.maxOffset;\n\n            if (!retainedRecords.isEmpty()) {\n                // we check if the delete horizon should be set to a new value\n                // in which case, we need to reset the base timestamp and overwrite the timestamp deltas\n                // if the batch does not contain tombstones, then we don't need to overwrite batch\n                boolean needToSetDeleteHorizon = (containsTombstones || containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) &&\n                    batch.deleteHorizonMs().isEmpty();\n                if (writeOriginalBatch && !needToSetDeleteHorizon) {\n                    batch.writeTo(bufferOutputStream);\n                    filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, retainedRecords.size(), false);\n                } else {\n                    long deleteHorizonMs;\n                    if (needToSetDeleteHorizon)\n                        deleteHorizonMs = filter.currentTime + filter.deleteRetentionMs;\n                    else\n                        deleteHorizonMs = batch.deleteHorizonMs().orElse(RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP);\n                    try (final MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = buildRetainedRecordsInto(batch, retainedRecords,\n                            bufferOutputStream, deleteHorizonMs)) {\n                        MemoryRecords records = builder.build();\n                        int filteredBatchSize = records.sizeInBytes();\n                        MemoryRecordsBuilder.RecordsInfo info = builder.info();\n                        filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(info.maxTimestamp, info.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp,\n                            maxOffset, retainedRecords.size(), filteredBatchSize);\n                    }\n                }\n            } else if (batchRetention == BatchRetention.RETAIN_EMPTY) {\n                if (batch.magic() < RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2) // should never happen\n                    throw new IllegalStateException(\"Empty batches are only supported for magic v2 and above\");\n\n                bufferOutputStream.ensureRemaining(DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD);\n                DefaultRecordBatch.writeEmptyHeader(bufferOutputStream.buffer(), RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, batch.producerId(),\n                        batch.producerEpoch(), batch.baseSequence(), batch.baseOffset(), batch.lastOffset(),\n                        batch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), batch.timestampType(), batch.maxTimestamp(),\n                        batch.isTransactional(), batch.isControlBatch());\n                filterResult.updateRetainedBatchMetadata(batch, 0, true);\n            }\n\n            // If we had to allocate a new buffer to fit the filtered buffer (see KAFKA-5316), return early to\n            // avoid the need for additional allocations.\n            ByteBuffer outputBuffer = bufferOutputStream.buffer();\n            if (outputBuffer != destinationBuffer) {\n                filterResult.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n                return filterResult;\n            }\n        }\n\n        return filterResult;\n    }\n\n    private static BatchFilterResult filterBatch(RecordBatch batch,\n                                                 BufferSupplier decompressionBufferSupplier,\n                                                 FilterResult filterResult,\n                                                 RecordFilter filter) {\n        try (final CloseableIterator<Record> iterator = batch.streamingIterator(decompressionBufferSupplier)) {\n            long maxOffset = -1;\n            boolean containsTombstones = false;\n            // Convert records with old record versions\n            boolean writeOriginalBatch = batch.magic() >= RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE;\n            List<Record> retainedRecords = new ArrayList<>();\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                Record record = iterator.next();\n                filterResult.messagesRead += 1;\n\n                if (filter.shouldRetainRecord(batch, record)) {\n                    if (record.offset() > maxOffset)\n                        maxOffset = record.offset();\n\n                    retainedRecords.add(record);\n\n                    if (!record.hasValue()) {\n                        containsTombstones = true;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    writeOriginalBatch = false;\n                }\n            }\n            return new BatchFilterResult(retainedRecords, writeOriginalBatch, containsTombstones, maxOffset);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static class BatchFilterResult {\n        private final List<Record> retainedRecords;\n        private final boolean writeOriginalBatch;\n        private final boolean containsTombstones;\n        private final long maxOffset;\n        private BatchFilterResult(List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                  final boolean writeOriginalBatch,\n                                  final boolean containsTombstones,\n                                  final long maxOffset) {\n            this.retainedRecords = retainedRecords;\n            this.writeOriginalBatch = writeOriginalBatch;\n            this.containsTombstones = containsTombstones;\n            this.maxOffset = maxOffset;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private static MemoryRecordsBuilder buildRetainedRecordsInto(RecordBatch originalBatch,\n                                                                 List<Record> retainedRecords,\n                                                                 ByteBufferOutputStream bufferOutputStream,\n                                                                 final long deleteHorizonMs) {\n        Compression compression = Compression.of(originalBatch.compressionType()).build();\n        // V0 has no timestamp type or timestamp, so we set the timestamp to CREATE_TIME and timestamp to NO_TIMESTAMP.\n        // Note that this differs from produce up-conversion where the timestamp type topic config is used and the log append\n        // time is generated if the config is LOG_APPEND_TIME. The reason for the different behavior is that there is\n        // no appropriate log append time we can generate at compaction time.\n        TimestampType timestampType = originalBatch.timestampType() == TimestampType.NO_TIMESTAMP_TYPE ?\n                TimestampType.CREATE_TIME : originalBatch.timestampType();\n        long logAppendTime = timestampType == TimestampType.LOG_APPEND_TIME ?\n                originalBatch.maxTimestamp() : RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        long baseOffset = originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2 ?\n                originalBatch.baseOffset() : retainedRecords.get(0).offset();\n\n        // Convert records with older record versions to the current one\n        MemoryRecordsBuilder builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferOutputStream, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE,\n                compression, timestampType, baseOffset, logAppendTime, originalBatch.producerId(),\n                originalBatch.producerEpoch(), originalBatch.baseSequence(), originalBatch.isTransactional(),\n                originalBatch.isControlBatch(), originalBatch.partitionLeaderEpoch(), bufferOutputStream.limit(), deleteHorizonMs);\n\n        for (Record record : retainedRecords)\n            builder.append(record);\n\n        if (originalBatch.magic() >= RecordBatch.MAGIC_VALUE_V2)\n            // we must preserve the last offset from the initial batch in order to ensure that the\n            // last sequence number from the batch remains even after compaction. Otherwise, the producer\n            // could incorrectly see an out of sequence error.\n            builder.overrideLastOffset(originalBatch.lastOffset());\n\n        return builder;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Get the byte buffer that backs this instance for reading.\n     */\n    public ByteBuffer buffer() {\n        return buffer.duplicate();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public MemoryRecords slice(int position, int size) {\n        if (position < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid position: \" + position + \" in read from \" + this);\n        if (position > buffer.limit())\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Slice from position \" + position + \" exceeds end position of \" + this);\n        if (size < 0)\n            throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"Invalid size: \" + size + \" in read from \" + this);\n\n        int availableBytes = Math.min(size, buffer.limit() - position);\n        // As of now, clients module support Java11 hence can't use ByteBuffer::slice(position, size) method.\n        // So we need to create a duplicate buffer and set the position and limit. Duplicate buffer\n        // is backed by original bytes hence not the content but only the relative position and limit\n        // are changed in the duplicate buffer. Once the position and limit are set, we can call the\n        // slice method to get the sliced buffer, which is a backed by the original buffer with the\n        // position reset to 0 and limit set to the size of the slice.\n        ByteBuffer slicedBuffer = buffer.duplicate();\n        slicedBuffer.position(position);\n        slicedBuffer.limit(position + availableBytes);\n        // Reset the position to 0 so that the sliced view has a relative position.\n        slicedBuffer = slicedBuffer.slice();\n\n        return readableRecords(slicedBuffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public Iterable<MutableRecordBatch> batches() {\n        return batches;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String toString() {\n        return \"MemoryRecords(size=\" + sizeInBytes() +\n                \", buffer=\" + buffer +\n                \")\";\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean equals(Object o) {\n        if (this == o)\n            return true;\n        if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass())\n            return false;\n\n        MemoryRecords that = (MemoryRecords) o;\n\n        return buffer.equals(that.buffer);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public int hashCode() {\n        return buffer.hashCode();\n    }\n\n    public abstract static class RecordFilter {\n        public final long currentTime;\n        public final long deleteRetentionMs;\n\n        public RecordFilter(final long currentTime, final long deleteRetentionMs) {\n            this.currentTime = currentTime;\n            this.deleteRetentionMs = deleteRetentionMs;\n        }\n\n        public static class BatchRetentionResult {\n            public final BatchRetention batchRetention;\n            public final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            public BatchRetentionResult(final BatchRetention batchRetention,\n                                        final boolean containsMarkerForEmptyTxn) {\n                this.batchRetention = batchRetention;\n                this.containsMarkerForEmptyTxn = containsMarkerForEmptyTxn;\n            }\n        }\n\n        public enum BatchRetention {\n            DELETE, // Delete the batch without inspecting records\n            RETAIN_EMPTY, // Retain the batch even if it is empty\n            DELETE_EMPTY  // Delete the batch if it is empty\n        }\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether the full batch can be discarded (i.e. whether we even need to\n         * check the records individually).\n         */\n        protected abstract BatchRetentionResult checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch batch);\n\n        /**\n         * Check whether a record should be retained in the log. Note that {@link #checkBatchRetention(RecordBatch)}\n         * is used prior to checking individual record retention. Only records from batches which were not\n         * explicitly discarded with {@link BatchRetention#DELETE} will be considered.\n         */\n        protected abstract boolean shouldRetainRecord(RecordBatch recordBatch, Record record);\n    }\n\n    public static class FilterResult {\n        private ByteBuffer outputBuffer;\n        private int messagesRead = 0;\n        // Note that `bytesRead` should contain only bytes from batches that have been processed, i.e. bytes from\n        // `messagesRead` and any discarded batches.\n        private int bytesRead = 0;\n        private int messagesRetained = 0;\n        private int bytesRetained = 0;\n        private long maxOffset = -1L;\n        private long maxTimestamp = RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP;\n        private long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = -1L;\n\n        private FilterResult(ByteBuffer outputBuffer) {\n            this.outputBuffer = outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(MutableRecordBatch retainedBatch, int numMessagesInBatch, boolean headerOnly) {\n            int bytesRetained = headerOnly ? DefaultRecordBatch.RECORD_BATCH_OVERHEAD : retainedBatch.sizeInBytes();\n            updateRetainedBatchMetadata(retainedBatch.maxTimestamp(), retainedBatch.lastOffset(),\n                    retainedBatch.lastOffset(), numMessagesInBatch, bytesRetained);\n        }\n\n        private void updateRetainedBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset,\n                                                 int messagesRetained, int bytesRetained) {\n            validateBatchMetadata(maxTimestamp, shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, maxOffset);\n            if (maxTimestamp > this.maxTimestamp) {\n                this.maxTimestamp = maxTimestamp;\n                this.shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp = shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n            }\n            this.maxOffset = Math.max(maxOffset, this.maxOffset);\n            this.messagesRetained += messagesRetained;\n            this.bytesRetained += bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        private void validateBatchMetadata(long maxTimestamp, long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp, long maxOffset) {\n            if (maxTimestamp != RecordBatch.NO_TIMESTAMP && shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"shallowOffset undefined for maximum timestamp \" + maxTimestamp);\n            if (maxOffset < 0)\n                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"maxOffset undefined\");\n        }\n\n        public ByteBuffer outputBuffer() {\n            return outputBuffer;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRead() {\n            return messagesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRead() {\n            return bytesRead;\n        }\n\n        public int messagesRetained() {\n            return messagesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public int bytesRetained() {\n            return bytesRetained;\n        }\n\n        public long maxOffset() {\n            return maxOffset;\n        }\n\n        public long maxTimestamp() {\n            return maxTimestamp;\n        }\n\n        public long shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp() {\n            return shallowOffsetOfMaxTimestamp;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords readableRecords(ByteBuffer buffer) {\n        return new MemoryRecords(buffer);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               Compression compression,\n                                               TimestampType timestampType,\n                                               long baseOffset) {\n        return builder(buffer, RecordBatch.CURRENT_MAGIC_VALUE, compression, timestampType, baseOffset);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n          RecordBatch.NO_PARTITION_LEADER_EPOCH);\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecordsBuilder builder(ByteBuffer buffer,\n                                               byte magic,\n static MemoryRecords withRecords(byte magic, long initialOffset, Compression compression,\n                                            TimestampType timestampType, SimpleRecord... records) {\n                             TimestampType timestampType, long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                            int baseSequence, int partitionLeaderEpoch, boolean isTransactional,\n                                            SimpleRecord... records) {\n        if (records.length == 0)\n            return MemoryRecords.EMPTY;\n        int sizeEstimate = AbstractRecords.estimateSizeInBytes(magic, compression.type(), Arrays.asList(records));\n        ByteBufferOutputStream bufferStream = new ByteBufferOutputStream(sizeEstimate);\n        long builder = new MemoryRecordsBuilder(bufferStream, magic, compression, timestampType,\n                initialOffset, logAppendTime, producerId, producerEpoch, baseSequence, isTransactional, false,\n                partitionLeaderEpoch, sizeEstimate)) {\n            for (SimpleRecord record : records)\n                builder.append(record);\n            return builder.build();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public static MemoryRecords withEndTransactionMarker(long producerId, short producerEpoch, EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        return withEndTransactionMarker(0L, System.currentTimeMillis(),                                        long producerId, short producerEpoch,\n                                                         EndTransactionMarker marker) {\n        int endTxnMarkerBatchSize implementation backed by a ByteBuffer. This is used only for reading or","- * modifying in-place an existing buffer of record batches. To create a new buffer see {@link MemoryRecordsBuilder},","- * or one of the {@link #builder(ByteBuffer, byte, Compression, TimestampType, long)} variants.","+ * In-memory collection of Kafka record batches backed by a ByteBuffer.","+ *","+ * <p><strong>Purpose:</strong>","+ * This class manages a contiguous buffer containing one or more {@link RecordBatch} objects,","+ * providing iteration, filtering, slicing, and serialization capabilities.
